# FreakJS

FreakJS is a Bun-native frontend framework with zero runtime npm dependencies.
File-based routing. SSR + client hydration. Edge-ready (Vercel Build Output API v3).
Free to deploy on Vercel, Netlify, Cloudflare Pages — no backend required.

Created by João Gabriel do Vale Souza & Lucas Silva dos Santos.
Repository: https://github.com/lucas-s-santos/FreakJS
License: MIT

---

## Install (one-time, global)

Windows:
  winget install freakjs

Mac/Linux:
  curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/lucas-s-santos/FreakJS/main/install.sh | bash

---

## Create a project

  freakjs create my-app
  cd my-app
  bun run dev      # → http://localhost:3000
  bun run build    # → .vercel/output/

---

## Project structure

  src/
    pages/           # file-based routes
      index.tsx      # → /
      about.tsx      # → /about
      blog/
        [slug].tsx   # → /blog/:slug  (dynamic)
        [...path].tsx # → /blog/:path* (catch-all)
      api/
        hello.ts     # → /api/hello   (Edge Function, no JSX)
    components/      # shared components
  public/            # static assets served at /
  package.json
  tsconfig.json

---

## Page component

Every file in src/pages/ must export a default function.
Props are typed with PageProps from "freakjs".

  import type { PageProps } from "freakjs";

  export const metadata = {
    title: "Page title",
    description: "SEO description",
  };

  export default function Home({ url, params, searchParams }: PageProps) {
    return (
      <main>
        <h1>Hello</h1>
      </main>
    );
  }

PageProps shape:
  {
    params: Record<string, string>   // dynamic route segments
    searchParams: URLSearchParams    // ?key=value
    url: URL                         // full URL object
  }

---

## Dynamic routes

  // src/pages/blog/[slug].tsx
  export default function Post({ params }: PageProps) {
    return <h1>{params.slug}</h1>;
  }

  // src/pages/docs/[...path].tsx  (catch-all)
  export default function Docs({ params }: PageProps) {
    return <p>{params.path}</p>;
  }

---

## API routes

Files under src/pages/api/ export named HTTP method handlers.
No JSX. Return Response objects.

  // src/pages/api/hello.ts
  export async function GET(req: Request, params: Record<string, string>): Promise<Response> {
    return Response.json({ message: "hello" });
  }

  export async function POST(req: Request): Promise<Response> {
    const body = await req.json();
    return Response.json({ received: body }, { status: 201 });
  }

  export async function DELETE(req: Request, params: Record<string, string>): Promise<Response> {
    return new Response(null, { status: 204 });
  }

Supported methods: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS.
Unhandled methods auto-return 405 Method Not Allowed.

---

## Components

Plain functions returning JSX. No special imports except types.

  // src/components/Button.tsx
  import type { VNode } from "freakjs";

  interface ButtonProps {
    label: string;
    href?: string;
    children?: VNode[];
  }

  export function Button({ label, href, children }: ButtonProps) {
    return (
      <a href={href} className="btn">
        {label}
        {children}
      </a>
    );
  }

---

## JSX rules

- Use className instead of class
- Use htmlFor instead of for
- style accepts an object: style={{ color: "red", fontSize: "1rem" }}
- Event handlers (onClick, onChange, etc.) are attached on the client only
- Fragments: import { Fragment } from "freakjs" and use <Fragment> or <>

---

## Raw HTML / CSS blocks

Use dangerouslySetInnerHTML for <style> tags and raw HTML:

  const css = `
    body { background: #000; color: #fff; }
    @keyframes fade { from { opacity: 0 } to { opacity: 1 } }
  `;

  export default function Page() {
    return (
      <Fragment>
        <style dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: css }} />
        <main class="container">...</main>
      </Fragment>
    );
  }

---

## Imports

  import type { PageProps, PageMetadata, VNode, FunctionComponent } from "freakjs";
  import { Fragment } from "freakjs";
  import { renderToString } from "freakjs/server";
  import { hydrate } from "freakjs/client";

---

## tsconfig.json (required)

  {
    "compilerOptions": {
      "target": "ESNext",
      "module": "ESNext",
      "moduleResolution": "Bundler",
      "jsx": "react-jsx",
      "jsxImportSource": "freakjs",
      "strict": true,
      "skipLibCheck": true,
      "types": ["bun-types"]
    },
    "include": ["src/**/*"]
  }

---

## Deploy to Vercel

  bun run build
  vercel deploy --prebuilt

Or connect the GitHub repo. Set build command: bun run build
The .vercel/output/ directory is auto-detected by Vercel.

Static pages → CDN edge (zero compute).
Dynamic pages + API routes → Edge Functions (global, isolated V8).

---

## Using Supabase (no backend needed)

  // src/pages/api/posts.ts
  import { createClient } from "@supabase/supabase-js";

  const supabase = createClient(process.env.SUPABASE_URL!, process.env.SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!);

  export async function GET(): Promise<Response> {
    const { data, error } = await supabase.from("posts").select("*");
    if (error) return Response.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 });
    return Response.json(data);
  }

---

## Design conventions

FreakJS projects use dark backgrounds by default.
Primary accent color: #7c3aed (purple).
Font stack: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif.
Monospace: 'SF Mono', 'Fira Code', 'Consolas', monospace.

---

## What FreakJS does NOT have (yet)

- No useState / reactive state (use vanilla JS with event handlers)
- No CSS modules (use inline styles or dangerouslySetInnerHTML <style>)
- No image optimization
- No middleware
